source_jobs.title: Chief of Staff
source_jobs.description:
At Alternative Payments, we are transforming the way service-based companies handle payments. Our innovative platform automates the entire accounts receivable process, helping businesses save time, reduce costs, and scale with confidence.

We are building a global team that values innovation, impact, and collaboration. As part of a scaling FinTech company, every person on our team has the opportunity to shape our future, influence our products, and make a real difference for our customers.

What We’re Looking For

Alternative Payments is looking for an outcome oriented Chief of Staff to join our rapidly growing team. The ideal candidate is a highly analytical problem solver, with critical thinking skills who can quickly understand the core drivers of the business. You will operate at the forefront of AI capabilities and tooling to develop and improve systems across core business functions. This role is part analyst, part strategist, part project manager, and part builder.

Reporting to the Chief Operating Officer you will work on complex business problems, analyzing and generating insights from data across multiple workstreams. You will plan, lead and execute operational and strategic planning initiatives that have a direct and quantifiable impact on the business

This is a hybrid role requiring candidates to be in our New York office 4x per week.

What You’ll Do

 - Work cross-functionally to execute on high-impact projects and implement new processes to scale operations.

 - Serve as a strategic partner, identifying issues, building models and analyzing data to make recommendations to improve financial and operational decision-making across the organization.

 - Leverage AI and automation to accelerate execution, including by not limited to Claude Code, Cursor, custom-built MCPs, etc.

 - Prepare regular reports and dashboards to communicate insights and recommendations to stakeholders.

 - Support the leadership team in executing all operational and strategic initiatives.

 - Monitor, manage and report on payment activity to create operating efficiency, optimize costs and mitigate risks

 - Support the sales + customer success teams with complex challenges

 - Identify inefficiencies across the organization and improve processes and / or create automations to eliminate the inefficiencies

What You’ll Bring

 - 3-6 years of professional experience within investment banking, private equity, or financial services

 - Superior analytical, critical thinking and quantitative skills

 - Intermediate knowledge of AI, prompting, key use cases and an affinity to learn about how to more deeply integrate AI into your daily workflows

 - Ability to execute in ambiguous, fast paced environments under tight and unpredictable timelines and produce accurate, high quality results

 - High sense of urgency, a bias for action and work ethic

 - Rigorous attention to detail

 - Ability to handle multiple competing projects

 - Ability to work independently and be proactive

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills

 - Strong problem solving and decision making skills

 - Ability to work collaboratively and cross functionally within the organization

Nice to Haves

 - Experience working at a high growth start up in the fintech space

 - At least 2 years experience working in investment banking

 - Experience leveraging Claude Code or similar AI applications

 - Computer science coursework / training at some point in career

What We Offer

 - Competitive salary tailored to your experience, skills, and expertise.
   
   - The total compensation range for this role is $150,000 - $200,000, incl. equity. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the approximate total target compensation for the position. Within the range, individual pay is determined by factors including relevant skills, experience, education/training.

 - Equity opportunities so you can share in our growth and success.

 - Unlimited PTO and flexibility when you ne

source_jobs.title: VP, Marketing
source_jobs.description:
Alternative Payments is building the financial operating system for SMBs, consolidating the disconnected tech stack that holds service-based businesses back. Starting with MSPs, we've spent five years perfecting accounts receivable, and we're now expanding into accounts payable, AI-powered analytics, and beyond. We're at an inflection point: closing the loop on money movement and laying the foundation for a platform that will reshape how service businesses operate financially.

We're growing fast, thinking big, and building a global team that wants to be part of something that lasts. We believe the best solutions come from diverse perspectives, deep collaboration, and a shared obsession with customer impact. If you're energized by real problems, real customers, and real impact, we want to hear from you!



WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We’re seeking a VP of Marketing to join our leadership team and build a scalable, revenue-driving marketing engine.

This role is ideal for a strategic and data-driven growth leader who thrives on full-funnel ownership, multi-vertical go-to-market expansion, and building high-performance teams. You’ll take a hands-on role in shaping our category narrative, driving qualified pipeline, and scaling marketing as a core growth driver of the business.

This is a hybrid role, requiring 3-4 days per week Downtown Toronto.



WHAT YOU’LL DO


OWN GO-TO-MARKET STRATEGY & MULTI-VERTICAL EXPANSION

 - Develop and execute ROI-driven, stage-gated market entry strategies across priority verticals including MSPs, accounting/CAS firms, commercial field services, staffing agencies, and wholesale distribution.

 - Define ICPs, vertical positioning, and messaging frameworks tailored to each segment.

 - Partner with the CEO and Sales leadership to align marketing investment with revenue targets.


BUILD AND SCALE DEMAND GENERATION

 - Design and scale a multi-channel demand engine across content, SEO, paid acquisition, ABM, email, and events.

 - Own full-funnel performance metrics including lead volume, SQL conversion, CAC, pipeline velocity, and marketing-sourced revenue.

 - Implement and optimize lead scoring, nurturing, and sales handoff workflows to maximize close rates.


ESTABLISH CATEGORY LEADERSHIP & BRAND AUTHORITY

 - Own and evolve the Alternative Payments brand narrative as the Financial Operating System for B2B service businesses.

 - Position the company as a differentiated alternative to legacy and embedded payment providers.

 - Lead PR, analyst relations, earned media, and thought leadership initiatives targeting financial decision-makers.


CONFERENCE & ECOSYSTEM STRATEGY

 - Develop and execute measurable event and conference strategies with clear pipeline attribution and ROI accountability.

 - Create structured pre-, during-, and post-event playbooks that drive qualified opportunities.

 - Represent Alternative Payments as a thought leader within priority industry ecosystems.


PARTNER & CHANNEL MARKETING

 - Build co-marketing programs with strategic ecosystem partners.

 - Develop enablement materials and referral activation strategies to expand partner-driven pipeline.

 - Identify and scale new partnership channels across accounting, field services, and related industries.


PRODUCT MARKETING & SALES ENABLEMENT

 - Translate product capabilities into compelling vertical-specific messaging.

 - Lead competitive intelligence and sales enablement initiatives including battle cards, ROI calculators, and vertical playbooks.

 - Partner with Product to inform roadmap prioritization based on market feedback.


MARKETING OPERATIONS & TEAM LEADERSHIP

 - Own marketing technology, attribution models, and performance reporting.

 - Manage marketing budget with ROI accountability, reallocating spend based on validated performance.

 - Build and lead a high-performance marketing team across demand generation, product marketing, content, events, and marketing operations.

 - Establish clear OKRs and foster a

source_jobs.title: Senior Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Alternative Payments is building the financial operating system for SMBs, consolidating the disconnected tech stack that holds service-based businesses back. Starting with MSPs, we've spent five years perfecting accounts receivable, and we're now expanding into accounts payable, AI-powered analytics, and beyond. We're at an inflection point: closing the loop on money movement and laying the foundation for a platform that will reshape how service businesses operate financially.

We're growing fast, thinking big, and building a global team that wants to be part of something that lasts. We believe the best solutions come from diverse perspectives, deep collaboration, and a shared obsession with customer impact. If you're energized by real problems, real customers, and real impact, we want to hear from you!




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We're seeking a Senior Platform Engineer to join our Engineering team and build a platform that simplifies the day-to-day of our engineers, driving operational excellence, enhancing infrastructure reliability and establishing cutting-edge observability across our critical systems.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a distributed systems environment, has strong opinions about platform development, and can move from strategy to execution quickly. The ideal candidate will have experience with AWS, Kubernetes, and agentic coding, being instrumental in how we drive innovation within our platform team, truly supporting the key stakeholders: our engineers.

You'll work closely with the Lead Platform Engineer and play a key role in building and improving our internal platform across the board. This role is available to candidates who are eligible to work in Brazil, Remote.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Design and implement scalable and secure infrastructure on AWS, ensuring high availability and reliability of our platform.

 - Develop and maintain distributed systems, with a focus on performance, scalability, and fault tolerance.

 - Collaborate with the engineering team (including our support engineers) to build and improve our internal platform, with a focus on automation, monitoring, and logging.

 - Work with Kubernetes to manage and orchestrate containerized applications, ensuring efficient resource utilization and high availability.

 - Participate in the development of our platform roadmap, ensuring that our technology stack aligns with our business goals and objectives.

 - Ensure that our platform is secure, compliant, and meets all relevant regulatory requirements.

 - Partner with the engineering team to identify and prioritize platform improvements, and to develop and implement solutions to address these needs.

 - Own the end-to-end platform development process, from design to delivery, ensuring that our platform meets the needs of our engineers and the company as a whole.


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 5+ years of experience in a platform engineering role, with a focus on distributed systems, AWS, and Kubernetes.

 - Strong experience with agentic coding and platform development, with a focus on building scalable and secure systems.

 - Experience with cloud-based infrastructure, including AWS (what we use), and a strong understanding of cloud security and compliance.

 - Experience with containerization and orchestration, using tools such as Docker and Kubernetes.

 - Proven experience in designing, implementing, and optimizing CI/CD pipelines, ideally with GitHub Actions.

 - In-depth experience with distributed systems, including design patterns, scalability, and fault tolerance.

 - A strong focus on automation, with experience using IaC tools such as Terraform, Ansible, or similar.

 - Experience with monitoring and logging tools, such as Datadog (what we use), New Relic, Splunk, Prometheus, Grafana, or similar.

 - A proactive mindset with the ability to solve complex problems, drive projects independently, and continuously innovate our reliability practices.

 - Strong communication skill

source_jobs.title: Head of Data
source_jobs.description:
JOB DESCRIPTION

Alternative Payments is building the financial operating system for SMBs, consolidating the disconnected tech stack that holds service-based businesses back. Starting with MSPs, we've spent five years perfecting accounts receivable, and we're now expanding into accounts payable, AI-powered analytics, and beyond. We're at an inflection point: closing the loop on money movement and laying the foundation for a platform that will reshape how service businesses operate financially.

We're growing fast, thinking big, and building a global team that wants to be part of something that lasts. We believe the best solutions come from diverse perspectives, deep collaboration, and a shared obsession with customer impact. If you're energized by real problems, real customers, and real impact, we want to hear from you!

What We’re Looking For

We’re seeking a Head of Data to join our Operations team and own the intelligence layer that sits across everything we build. Alternative Payments sits on a substantial and strategically valuable data asset, accounting data, payment data, and rich operational data from across the business.

This is a player-coach role. You will set the architecture vision and own the data roadmap, but you will also roll up your sleeves by reviewing and contributing to code, solving data quality issues, and working directly with the data engineering team.

This role is available to candidates eligible to work in the US, hybrid in New York.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

A key challenge of this role is balancing reactive work (data quality, stakeholder requests, system syncs) against executing the longer-term data strategy. You will own:


DATA STRATEGY & INFRASTRUCTURE

 - Own the full data infrastructure and architecture: pipelines, data models, gold/silver/bronze layer design, and real-time vs. batch tradeoffs.

 - Develop a clear, actionable data strategy and own the process of getting buy-in across leadership, engineering, and product to execute it.

 - Bridge the gap between technical data engineering and business context, translating leadership’s strategic questions into queryable, actionable data products.

 - Establish data governance, quality standards, and observability practices; ensure data lineage, security, and quality control are baked into the architecture, not added as an afterthought.

 - Lead complex data projects requiring entity resolution and enrichment at scale, using deterministic rules, fuzzy matching, and ML-based approaches to unify multi-source data

 - Prepare datasets and feature pipelines for machine learning workflows; prototype and implement predictive models that leverage unified datasets.


ANALYTICS & SELF-SERVE INSIGHTS

 - Move the organization beyond dashboards toward self-serve analytics, insight generation, and data-driven recommendations that are accessible to humans, agents, and partners.

 - Own data across both product data (what users do in the platform) and operational data (Salesforce, Zendesk, internal tooling), ensuring both feed into a coherent intelligence layer.

 - Own the data product roadmap for external-facing analytics from scoping and architecture through to delivery, coordinating across data engineering and product to ship partner-facing features that surface clean, structured, and actionable insights.

 - This includes leading complex, cross-functional data projects end-to-end: defining requirements, driving alignment, and ensuring the underlying data layer is accurate, performant, and built for non-technical consumption at scale.


AI COLLABORATION

 - Ensure the right data is available, clean, and structured so the AI engineering function can build and ship AI features confidently.

 - Build toward a data layer that is AI-queryable: structure data so it can be consumed by agents, embedding models, and LLM-powered retrieval systems and find the right balance between unstructured and structured data.

 - Collaborate closely with the engineering and ops team

source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
Alternative Payments is building the financial operating system for SMBs, consolidating the disconnected tech stack that holds service-based businesses back. Starting with MSPs, we've spent five years perfecting accounts receivable, and we're now expanding into accounts payable, AI-powered analytics, and beyond. We're at an inflection point: closing the loop on money movement and laying the foundation for a platform that will reshape how service businesses operate financially.

We're growing fast, thinking big, and building a global team that wants to be part of something that lasts. We believe the best solutions come from diverse perspectives, deep collaboration, and a shared obsession with customer impact. If you're energized by real problems, real customers, and real impact, we want to hear from you!



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

We are seeking a driven, coachable, and competitive Business Development Representative (BDR) to join our team. Our BDRs are the engine of our outbound growth and this is a hands-on prospecting role focused on creating pipelines from scratch.

You will identify and create new business opportunities through high-volume outbound outreach, develop strong discovery and messaging skills, and help us build a repeatable outbound playbook as we scale. This role is ideal for someone who wants to master outbound sales and grow into a future AE or revenue leadership path.

Candidates must be located in the GTA, as this is a hybrid position requiring candidates to come into the Downtown Toronto office 3-4 days a week (Mon - Wed+).



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Outbound Pipeline Creation: Own prospecting into targeted accounts through cold calling (80 - 100/day), email, and social outreach. Research accounts, personalize messaging, and consistently generate qualified meetings that convert into pipeline.

 - Structured Execution: Operate with discipline and consistency against daily activity targets. Maintain accurate CRM hygiene, track performance metrics, and continuously test and refine outreach strategies.

 - Product & Industry Fluency: Develop a strong understanding of Alternative Payments’ platform and the Managed Service Provider (MSP) ecosystem. Stay curious about fintech trends and emerging tools (including AI!) that improve outbound effectiveness.

 - Sales Strategy Collaboration: Work closely with Account Executives and sales leadership to refine messaging, share market feedback, and contribute to scalable outbound strategies.

 - Team Contribution: Participate actively in coaching, training, and peer collaboration to continuously elevate individual and team performance.
   


WHAT YOU WILL BRING (REQUIRED)


Core Attributes

 - High internal drive and competitiveness, with comfort handling rejection and high outbound volume

 - Strong coachability and willingness to implement feedback quickly

 - Clear, confident written and verbal communication skills

 - Organized, systems-oriented approach to managing work and data



EXPERIENCE: WE ARE OPEN TO TWO PROFILES


Option A: Early Sales Experience (1–2 years)

 - Experience in outbound sales, SDR/BDR, or related roles

 - Track record of meeting or exceeding activity or meeting targets

 - Experience maintaining accurate CRM records


NICE TO HAVE:

 - Experience in fintech, payments, or the MSP ecosystem

 - Startup or high-growth company experience

 - Familiarity with modern sales tools or AI-assisted prospecting workflows

Option B: High-Drive Career Starter

 - Experience in high-performance or customer-facing environments (e.g., athletics, hospitality, recruitment, or similar)

 - Demonstrated grit, consistency, and goal achievement

 - Strong interest in building a long-term career in sales and fintech

We value mindset, work ethic, and growth potential as much as prior experience.



COMPENSATION 

Total compensation range for this role is $80k CAD 

($60k base + $20K uncapped bonus). The range displayed on each job posting reflects the approximate

source_jobs.title: Partnerships Lead (Vancouver)
source_jobs.description:
Alternative Payments is building the financial operating system for SMBs, consolidating the disconnected tech stack that holds service-based businesses back. Starting with MSPs, we've spent five years perfecting accounts receivable, and we're now expanding into accounts payable, AI-powered analytics, and beyond. We're at an inflection point: closing the loop on money movement and laying the foundation for a platform that will reshape how service businesses operate financially.

We're growing fast, thinking big, and building a global team that wants to be part of something that lasts. We believe the best solutions come from diverse perspectives, deep collaboration, and a shared obsession with customer impact. If you're energized by real problems, real customers, and real impact, we want to hear from you!

What We’re Looking For

We're seeking a Partnerships Lead (ERP focus) to join our Partnerships team and aggressively expand our Enterprise Resource Planning Partner (ERP) Program across North America.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives on building relationships from scratch, loves the thrill of the hunt, and wants to take full ownership of a high-impact, greenfield partnership motion — from cold outreach to active referral-generating relationships. You won't be inheriting a warm book of business. You'll be building one.

This role is available to candidates eligible to work in Canada in Toronto or Vancouver. Regular travel is required for this position.



What You’ll Do

 - Own the ERP Partner motion end-to-end — from cold outreach to ERP implementers through onboarding, activation, and ongoing relationship management.

 - Develop deep familiarity with the Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central, NetSuite, and Sage ecosystems and the implementor community within each.

 - Run structured activation sequences with new partners from first outreach through active referral generation.

 - Represent Alternative Payments at key ERP industry conferences and events, including Directions North America and Community Summit.

 - Collaborate with internal Sales and Marketing teams to maximize the impact of ERP partnerships on new business.

 - Propose improvements and help establish a repeatable ERP partnership motion that can scale across new ecosystems.

 - Mentor and support team members, sharing knowledge and fostering growth.

What You’ll Bring

 - 2+ years of experience in SaaS sales and/or partnerships.

 - Proven track record in outbound sales motions — cold outreach, pipeline generation, and building relationships from zero.

 - Familiarity with ERP ecosystems such as Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central, NetSuite, or Sage — existing relationships with ERP implementers are a strong plus.

 - Strong communication and relationship-building skills, with the EQ to develop complex, trust-based partnerships over time.

 - A proactive, highly organized mindset with the ability to operate independently and solve problems without a playbook.

Nice to Haves

 - Familiarity with ERP ecosystems such as Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central, NetSuite, or Sage — existing relationships with ERP implementers are a strong plus.

 - Experience in FinTech, payments, startups, or similarly fast-moving environments.

 - Entrepreneurial background or evidence of building something from scratch.

 - Comfort representing a company at industry conferences and events.

 - Track record of mentoring, leading projects, or driving organizational improvements.

 - Comfort working in fast-paced, dynamic, and high-impact environments.

What We Offer

 - Competitive salary tailored to your experience, skills, and expertise.
   
   - The total compensation range (OTE) for this role is $80,000 - $100,000CAD, plus equity. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the approximate total target compensation for the position. Within the range, individual pay is determined by factors including relevant skills, experience, education/training.

 - Equi

source_jobs.title: Creative Director
source_jobs.description:
Alternative Payments is building the financial operating system for SMBs, consolidating the disconnected tech stack that holds service-based businesses back. Starting with MSPs, we've spent five years perfecting accounts receivable, and we're now expanding into accounts payable, AI-powered analytics, and beyond. We're at an inflection point: closing the loop on money movement and laying the foundation for a platform that will reshape how service businesses operate financially.

We're growing fast, thinking big, and building a global team that wants to be part of something that lasts. We believe the best solutions come from diverse perspectives, deep collaboration, and a shared obsession with customer impact. If you're energized by real problems, real customers, and real impact, we want to hear from you!




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We’re seeking a Creative Director to join our Marketing team and help us define how Alternative Payments shows up across every touchpoint—products, verticals, channels, and campaigns.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives on strategic thinking, customer insight, cross-functional collaboration, and wants to take a hands-on role in shaping messaging architecture, guiding communication strategies, and elevating content quality across the organization.

This is a hybrid position based Downtown Toronto and is available to candidates who are eligible to work in Canada.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Lead and execute on content strategy initiatives across products (AR, AP, Analytics), verticals (MSP, Accounting, and beyond), and channels from research to rollout.

 - Conduct and synthesize customer, market, and competitive research to inform messaging, positioning, and content direction.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams including Product, Sales, Customer Success, Performance Marketing, and Design to deliver cohesive, high-impact messaging.

 - Own messaging architecture, including brand narrative, product positioning, audience personas, and communications strategies.

 - Establish and evolve brand voice, editorial standards, and content quality frameworks across the organization.

 - Provide strategic direction on channel strategy and content playbooks for product and vertical launches.

 - Manage and mentor 2 creative team members across design and multimedia, elevating content quality and fostering a culture of strong storytelling and strategic thinking.


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 8+ years of experience in content strategy, brand strategy, or a related field within B2B SaaS, FinTech, or a high-growth startup environment.

 - Strong expertise in customer research, messaging frameworks, and positioning strategy.

 - Proven experience owning and driving content strategy at a company level (not just execution).

 - Strong communication skills to collaborate effectively across Product, Marketing, Sales, and executive stakeholders.

 - A proactive mindset with the ability to operate autonomously, make strategic decisions, and influence outcomes.

 - Experience developing brand voice, editorial standards, and scalable messaging systems.


NICE TO HAVES

 - Experience in FinTech, payments, or multi-product SaaS environments.

 - Familiarity with multi-product and multi-vertical go-to-market strategies.

 - Track record of mentoring teams and elevating content quality across functions.

 - Experience working alongside multimedia, performance marketing, and design teams.

 - Comfort working in fast-paced, dynamic, and high-impact environments.


WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary tailored to your experience, skills, and expertise.
   
   - The total compensation range for this role is $125,000 – $150,000 CAD, plus equity. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the approximate total target compensation for the position. Within the range, individual pay is determined by factors including relevant skills, experience, education/training.

 - Equity opportunities so you can share in our growth and succe

source_jobs.title: Director of Field Sales
source_jobs.description:
Alternative Payments is building the financial operating system for SMBs, consolidating the disconnected tech stack that holds service-based businesses back. Starting with MSPs, we’ve spent five years perfecting accounts receivable, and we’re now expanding into accounts payable, AI-powered analytics, and beyond. We’re at an inflection point: closing the loop on money movement and laying the foundation for a platform that will reshape how service businesses operate financially.

We’re growing fast, thinking big, and building a global team that wants to be part of something that lasts. We believe the best solutions come from diverse perspectives, deep collaboration, and a shared obsession with customer impact. If you’re energized by real problems, real customers, and real impact, we want to hear from you!



WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We’re seeking a Director of Field Sales to own in-person event strategy and execution across the MSP industry. You’ll sell at conferences and events, build the system around them, and develop the AE team into strong conference sellers who can eventually run the playbook without you.

The other half of the job is coaching. You set the standard for how AP shows up at a conference — how to work a room, how to run a booth conversation that leads somewhere, how to present on stage, how to turn a badge scan into a qualified meeting. You bring AEs to events, coach them in real time, and debrief afterward. Over time, the team should be able to execute the playbook without you at every event.

Community matters in this role. The MSP world runs on relationships and referrals. You’ll build a personal network within these communities that generates warm introductions, referral pipeline, and the kind of trust that makes people take a meeting because they know you, not because they got a cold email.

NOTE: This is a fully remote position, open to folks currently living in the US or Canada.
This role requires significant travel, including quarterly on-site visits to the Toronto office.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own in-person events strategy across the MSP industry: which events we attend, what our commercial plan is at each, and how we measure success.

 - Execute at events personally: booth selling, stage presentations, executive dinners, hallway conversations, and after-hours relationship building.

 - Build the pre-event and post-event system: targeted outreach before each conference, structured follow-up afterward, and pipeline tracking tied to specific events.

 - Develop AEs into conference sellers. Bring them to events, coach them on-site, debrief afterward, and build their confidence at in-person pipeline generation.

 - Experiment with new event formats and tactics: different booth approaches, dinner structures, partnership activations, and regional meetup strategies. Measure results and iterate.

 - Build a personal network within the MSP communities that generates referral pipeline and warm introductions.

 - Collaborate with the Event Manager on logistics and the Director of Revenue Enablement on training materials, but own the commercial playbook and on-site execution.

 - Report on event ROI with real numbers: pipeline generated, meetings booked, deals influenced, cost per opportunity.


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 5–10 years in B2B SaaS or fintech sales with significant in-person and field selling experience.

 - Familiarity with the MSP ecosystem and IT channel events.

 - You’ve worked conferences, trade shows, and executive events — not just attended them. You know the difference between showing up and executing.

 - You’ve presented on stage to a room of 50–500 people and you’re good at it.

 - A track record of building relationships that turn into pipeline — the kind where people take your call because they met you at an event two years ago.

 - You’ve coached or mentored junior sellers on in-person selling skills, formally or informally.

 - You’re systematic about events: you plan before, execute during

source_jobs.title: Applied AI Lead
source_jobs.description:
Alternative Payments is building the financial operating system for SMBs, consolidating the disconnected tech stack that holds service-based businesses back. Starting with MSPs, we've spent five years perfecting accounts receivable, and we're now expanding into accounts payable, AI-powered analytics, and beyond. We're at an inflection point: closing the loop on money movement and laying the foundation for a platform that will reshape how service businesses operate financially.

We're growing fast, thinking big, and building a global team that wants to be part of something that lasts. We believe the best solutions come from diverse perspectives, deep collaboration, and a shared obsession with customer impact. If you're energized by real problems, real customers, and real impact, we want to hear from you!


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We’re seeking an Applied AI Lead to join our team and own the AI layer powering our internal operations.

This is not a typical AI role. You’ll work alongside our bizops, finance, support and engineering teams to identify where AI can eliminate manual work, reduce cycle time and surface better decisions. You’ll also serve as the technical guardrail for AI work happening across the company, setting the architecture, security standards and integration patterns that let teams build with confidence.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives on technical problem-solving with real business context, and wants to take a hands-on role in shaping how an AI-native fintech platform gets built from the inside out.

This role is available to candidates eligible to work in the US, hybrid in New York.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Act as the operational owner of AI at the business; partner with functional leaders to identify high-leverage areas where AI tooling can dramatically reduce effort, increase quality, or accelerate timelines

 - Stand up agentic workflows end-to-end using Claude Code (or similarly capable models), our internal stack, MCPs, etc.

 - Own context engineering, prompt design, tool definitions, eval harnesses, and human-in-the-loop checkpoints.

 - Build and maintain reusable skills, sub-agents, and MCP integrations that compound across functions

 - Own AP's internal AI tool stack: model selection, vendor evaluation, build-vs-buy decisions, license utilization, data handling policies, and access controls.

 - Support the biz-ops and operational engineering team in building AI-native dashboards and workflows that are architected for scale.

 - Act as a resource on AI security across the organization: prompt injection defense, access controls in agentic systems, vulnerability assessment, and staying current on emerging AI attack surfaces.

 - Collaborate with the Head of Data to ensure data infrastructure supports AI consumption patterns and advocate for what needs to change to unlock specific product bets.

 - Help raise AI literacy across the organization and facilitate training sessions.

 - Support product organization on AI feature implementation: define context structure, retrieval strategy, and how agents interact with platform data.

 - Propose and help establish AI best practices, tooling standards, and evaluation frameworks across both product and operational contexts.


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 6+ years of operating experience in biz ops, strategy, transformation, consulting, software development, data or a similar outcome-owning role with meaningful depth in AI systems: you’ve built and shipped production AI, not just used it.

 - Production prompt engineering and context engineering experience: You can articulate the trade-offs between long context, RAG, fine-tuning, and tool use, and have made these calls before.

 - MCP experience: you've built MCP servers, integrated them into agent workflows, and understand the design space.

 - Strong understanding of AI security: prompt injection, access control in agentic systems, data exposure risks, and how to harden LLM-adjacent infrastructure.

 - The

source_jobs.title: Head of Product
source_jobs.description:
Alternative Payments is building the financial operating system for SMBs, consolidating the disconnected tech stack that holds service-based businesses back. Starting with MSPs, we've spent five years perfecting accounts receivable, and we're now expanding into accounts payable, AI-powered analytics, and beyond. We're at an inflection point: closing the loop on money movement and laying the foundation for a platform that will reshape how service businesses operate financially.

We're growing fast, thinking big, and building a global team that wants to be part of something that lasts. We believe the best solutions come from diverse perspectives, deep collaboration, and a shared obsession with customer impact. If you're energized by real problems, real customers, and real impact, we want to hear from you!


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We’re seeking a Head of Product to join our Product team and own the full product vision across our growing AI platform, spanning accounts receivable, accounts payable, and analytics.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives on cross-functional ownership and technical depth, and wants to take a hands-on role in shaping product strategy, raising the bar for the PM team, and driving innovation that connects data, AI, and payments into a coherent product experience.

This role is available to candidates eligible to work in New York or Toronto, hybrid.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own the product roadmap across AR, AP, and analytics: understanding how these products intersect and where the gaps create friction for customers and engineering.

 - Manage and grow a team of product managers and product designers, overseeing product design scope across the organisation.

 - Work closely with PMs to sharpen PRDs and bring technical rigour to requirements, acting as the bridge between product and engineering when needed.

 - Define and own the future product vision for Alternative Payments, synthesising market signals, customer insight, and company strategy into a coherent roadmap.

 - Implement AI thoughtfully across the product organization, not just for workflow efficiency, but as a genuine product capability that creates value for partners.

 - Propose and establish best practices for how the product team operates: discovery, prioritization, documentation, and cross-functional collaboration.


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 10+ years of product experience, with meaningful time in financial technology (payments, lending, or money movement) or a vertical SaaS company with large payments revenue contribution.

 - Proven experience managing product managers and designers across a multi-product portfolio.

 - Technical enough to read a system design doc, challenge an engineering estimate, and help PMs write PRDs that engineering teams trust.

 - Fluency with AI as a product capability: you can articulate how you’ve implemented AI in a product org, not just team tooling, but as a product surface.

 - Conceptual depth with AI technology: familiarity with embeddings, RAG, and agent-based architectures at a level that earns engineering credibility.

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills; able to write clean strategy docs and run tight leadership reviews.


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience managing design across an organization (product design + marketing design + sales collateral).

 - Prior experience transitioning product ownership from a founder — navigating that trust-building and handover process.

 - Track record of raising the technical floor in a PM team through coaching, process changes, or targeted hiring.

 - Comfort working in a fast-paced, high-ownership environment where the roadmap evolves quickly.


WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary tailored to your experience, skills, and expertise.
   
   - The base salary range for this role is $160K–$200K, plus equity. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the approximate total target compensation for the position. Within the range, individual pay is determi

source_jobs.title: Head of Outbound
source_jobs.description:
Alternative Payments is building the financial operating system for SMBs, consolidating the disconnected tech stack that holds service-based businesses back. Starting with MSPs, we’ve spent five years perfecting accounts receivable, and we’re now expanding into accounts payable, AI-powered analytics, and beyond. We’re at an inflection point: closing the loop on money movement and laying the foundation for a platform that will reshape how service businesses operate financially.

We’re growing fast, thinking big, and building a global team that wants to be part of something that lasts. We believe the best solutions come from diverse perspectives, deep collaboration, and a shared obsession with customer impact. If you’re energized by real problems, real customers, and real impact, we want to hear from you!



WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We’re seeking a Head of Outbound to own the strategy, execution, and accountability of our outbound sales motion across the MSP and Accounting segments. You’ll design the BDR outbound architecture — signal-based targeting, sequence strategy, cold call frameworks, and email positioning — then build rigor and accountability throughout the team to make sure it runs at a high level every day.

Our BDR team operates in segment-specific pods (SMB, Mid-Market, Accounting), each led by an AE team lead who handles coaching and deal-level training but isn’t directly responsible for day-to-day outbound accountability. Your job is to fill that gap: build what the AE leads teach, set the standard for outbound quality, and drive efficiency improvements that show up in the numbers.

This is a hybrid position requiring a minimum of 3 days in office based in Toronto, ON and is available to candidates who are eligible to work in Canada.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Improve outbound efficiency across MSP and Accounting segments: diagnose what’s working, redesign what isn’t, and raise the bar on BDR output.

 - Design BDR outbound playbooks for each segment: signal-based targeting, sequence architecture, cold call scripts, and email positioning tailored to different buyer personas.

 - Build rigor and accountability across the BDR team — daily/weekly operating cadences, activity standards, pipeline inspection, and performance management.

 - Collaborate across Marketing, Partnerships, and Events to drive a strategic approach to outbound efforts.

 - Work with Head of New Markets to build GTM plans for new verticals: define the buyer, the pitch, the outbound motion, and the pipeline targets.

 - Build the mid-market and enterprise outbound motion alongside the existing SMB process.


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 5–10 years in B2B SaaS or fintech sales, including time where your job was building or running outbound programs across multiple segments.

 - Experience building outbound playbooks: signal-based targeting, cold sequences, call frameworks. You know the difference between a sequence that books meetings and one that gets ignored.

 - A track record of improving BDR performance through better process, accountability, and outbound strategy — not just managing reps, but raising the standard.

 - You’ve worked at a company in the $3M–$20M ARR range where not everything was built yet.

 - Working knowledge of Salesforce, Apollo, Instantly.

 - Proven ability to leverage AI to drive team efficiency and prove outcomes with tangible results.


NICE TO HAVES

 - Familiarity with the MSP / Accounting ecosystem, PE-backed roll-ups, or payments/AR automation.

 - Experience building outbound motions across multiple verticals simultaneously.

 - Comfort with outbound tooling beyond Apollo — intent data platforms, enrichment tools, deliverability monitoring.

 - Track record coaching or mentoring BDRs into AE roles.

 - Deals you personally closed in markets your company hadn’t sold into before.


WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary tailored to your experience, skills, and expertise.

 - The total compensation range for this role is $185,000

source_jobs.title: Business Operations & Strategy Associate
source_jobs.description:
Alternative Payments is building the financial operating system for SMBs, consolidating the disconnected tech stack that holds service-based businesses back. Starting with MSPs, we've spent five years perfecting accounts receivable, and we're now expanding into accounts payable, AI-powered analytics, and beyond. We're at an inflection point: closing the loop on money movement and laying the foundation for a platform that will reshape how service businesses operate financially.

We're growing fast, thinking big, and building a global team that wants to be part of something that lasts. We believe the best solutions come from diverse perspectives, deep collaboration, and a shared obsession with customer impact. If you're energized by real problems, real customers, and real impact, we want to hear from you!




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Alternative Payments is looking for an outcome oriented Business Operations & Strategy Associate to join our rapidly growing team. The ideal candidate is a highly analytical problem solver, with critical thinking skills who can quickly understand the core drivers of the business. You will operate at the forefront of AI capabilities and tooling to develop and improve systems across core business functions. This role is part analyst, part strategist, part project manager, and part builder.

Reporting to the Chief Operating Officer you will work on complex business problems, analyzing and generating insights from data across multiple workstreams. You will plan, lead and execute operational and strategic planning initiatives that have a direct and quantifiable impact on the business



This is a hybrid role requiring candidates to be in our New York office 4x per week.





WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Work cross-functionally to execute on high-impact projects and implement new processes to scale operations.

 - Serve as a strategic partner, identifying issues, building models and analyzing data to make recommendations to improve financial and operational decision-making across the organization.

 - Leverage AI and automation to accelerate execution, including by not limited to Claude Code, Cursor, custom-built MCPs, etc.

 - Prepare regular reports and dashboards to communicate insights and recommendations to stakeholders.

 - Support the leadership team in executing all operational and strategic initiatives.

 - Monitor, manage and report on payment activity to create operating efficiency, optimize costs and mitigate risks

 - Support the sales + customer success teams with complex challenges

 - Identify inefficiencies across the organization and improve processes and / or create automations to eliminate the inefficiencies
   
   


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 2-5 years of professional experience within investment banking, private equity, or financial services

 - Superior analytical, critical thinking and quantitative skills

 - Intermediate knowledge of AI, prompting, key use cases and an affinity to learn about how to more deeply integrate AI into your daily workflows

 - Ability to execute in ambiguous, fast paced environments under tight and unpredictable timelines and produce accurate, high quality results

 - High sense of urgency, a bias for action and work ethic

 - Rigorous attention to detail

 - Ability to handle multiple competing projects

 - Ability to work independently and be proactive

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills

 - Strong problem solving and decision making skills

 - Ability to work collaboratively and cross functionally within the organization
   
   


NICE TO HAVES

 - Experience working at a high growth start up in the fintech space

 - At least 2 years experience working in investment banking

 - Experience leveraging Claude Code or similar AI applications

 - Computer science coursework / training at some point in career
   
   


WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary tailored to your experience, skills, and expertise.

source_jobs.title: Lead Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Alternative Payments is building the financial operating system for SMBs, consolidating the disconnected tech stack that holds service-based businesses back. Starting with MSPs, we've spent five years perfecting accounts receivable, and we're now expanding into accounts payable, AI-powered analytics, and beyond. We're at an inflection point: closing the loop on money movement and laying the foundation for a platform that will reshape how service businesses operate financially.

We're growing fast, thinking big, and building a global team that wants to be part of something that lasts. We believe the best solutions come from diverse perspectives, deep collaboration, and a shared obsession with customer impact. If you're energized by real problems, real customers, and real impact, we want to hear from you!




WHAT YOU'LL DO

We're looking for a Lead Software Engineer who's as comfortable talking about how a business makes money as they are designing a distributed system. This is a hands-on technical leadership role: you'll own initiatives end-to-end, partner closely with product, and help set the technical direction for a team that moves fast and operates with high autonomy.

You'll report to our VP of Engineering and work in a flat structure where ICs lead projects, not just contribute to them. We run parallel initiatives at any given time in small, two-person tribe structures. We expect the people who join us to be the kind who step up, make decisions, and take accountability for outcomes, technical and otherwise.

 - Own complex technical initiatives from conception to delivery, including architecture decisions, implementation, and cross-functional coordination with product and business stakeholders.

 - Lead cross-functional efforts (tiger teams and initiative-based squads) and serve as the technical point of accountability for the initiatives you're running.

 - Design and implement scalable microservices solutions, with particular attention to reliability, performance, and maintainability in a high-volume financial transactions environment.

 - Partner with the product to understand the business context and translate it into technical decisions. This means understanding why something matters to the business, not just what needs to be built.

 - Raise the technical bar across the team through code review, architecture feedback, and knowledge sharing with less senior engineers.

 - Leverage AI tooling as a core part of how you work. We're an AI-forward team and expect you to have a sophisticated, evolved setup, not just basic usage.


WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - 8+ years of software engineering experience, ideally including time at a fintech, high-growth startup, or company operating at scale.

 - Proficiency in Golang, Rust or openness to work with it.

 - Experience designing and evolving scalable architectures — microservices, distributed systems, or monoliths — with a clear point of view on the tradeoffs of each.

 - Hands-on experience with Kubernetes, PostgreSQL, and cloud platforms (we're on AWS).

 - Demonstrated ability to lead a project from soup to nuts. This means technical execution, stakeholder communication, and delivery accountability.

 - Business acumen: you understand how software products make money, you care about it, and it informs how you make technical decisions.

 - English communication skills sufficient to collaborate effectively with our teams in the US, Canada, and Brazil (our engineering team is based in Brazil!).

 - Experience in fintech or payments is a strong plus, but the more important signal is whether you've worked somewhere that expected you to understand the full picture.


NICE-TO-HAVES

 - Background as an Engineering Manager, Staff Engineer, Lead Engineer or Tech Lead with a desire to return to hands-on technical work.

 - Experience at companies serving US-based markets. Familiarity with the communication norms and business context that come with that.

 - Comfort with event-driven archi

notes: High-growth FinTech company with a strong focus on product development and innovation.
